I couldn't find any information on the plant Salaxis parviflora. However, I can provide information on the plant Salvias, which includes Salaxis.

Salvias are a genus of flowering plants in the mint family (Lamiaceae). The leaves and flowers of Salvias, such as Salvia parviflora, have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic effects, which may help to reduce stress and promote digestive health.

The bioactive constituents of Salvias, including Salvia parviflora, include flavonoids, phenolic acids, terpenoids, and rosmarinic acid, which are responsible for their medicinal properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Salvias include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote relaxation, alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression, and support immune function.
